$$N(t) = N_0 + n_b(t)$$

By definition of \(M_n\):

$$M_n(t) = \dfrac{M_0 \cdot c_0}{N(t)}$$

where \(M_0\) is the monomer molar mass and \(c_0\) is the initial molar concentration of repeat units. Differentiating with respect to time:

$$\frac{dM_n}{dt} = -\dfrac{M_0 c_0}{N(t)^2} \cdot \frac{dN}{dt} = -\dfrac{M_n(t)}{N(t)} \cdot k' \cdot [\text{Ester}](t)$$

In excess water (pseudo-first-order regime), \([\text{Ester}](t) \approx [\text{Ester}]_0\) and we may set the proportionality constant \(\kappa = k' \cdot [\text{Ester}]_0 \). Then:

$$\frac{dM_n}{dt} = -\kappa \cdot M_n(t)$$

Integrating from \(t = 0\) to \(t\):

$$\int_{M_{n,0}}^{M_n(t)} \dfrac{dM_n}{M_n} = -\kappa \int_0^t dt$$

$$\ln \left(\dfrac{M_n(t)}{M_{n,0}}\right) = -\kappa \, t$$

$$\boxed{M_n(t) = M_{n,0} \cdot e^{-\kappa t}}$$

Therefore the **molecular-weight half-life** is:

$$t_{1/2} = \dfrac{\ln 2}{\kappa}$$

> [!NOTE]
> **Interpretation for Examiners**: Notice the linear relationship between \(\ln(M_n)\) and \(t\). Plotting this is the standard KTU-laboratory method for extracting \(\kappa\) and hence the activation energy \(E_a\) via Arrhenius analysis.

### 3.2 Worked Analytical Example — Hydrolysis of a PLA Specimen

**Given Data:**
- \(M_{n,0} = 100{,}000\) g/mol
- \(\kappa = 1.0 \times 10^{-3}\) day⁻¹
- Test medium: phosphate buffer pH 7.4, 37 °C

**Step 1 — Compute molecular weight after 90 days**

$$\begin{aligned}
M_n(90) &= 100{,}000 \cdot e^{-(1.0 \times 10^{-3})(90)} \\
&= 100{,}000 \cdot e^{-0.09} \\
&= 100{,}000 \cdot 0.9139 \\
&= 91{,}393 \; \text{g/mol}
\end{aligned}$$

**Step 2 — Compute half-life**

$$t_{1/2} = \dfrac{\ln 2}{1.0 \times 10^{-3}} = 693 \; \text{days} \approx 1.9 \; \text{years}$$

**Step 3 — Effect of temperature rise to 55 °C**

If \(E_a = 80\) kJ/mol and the rate at 37 °C is \(\kappa_{37}\), then using the Arrhenius ratio:

$$\begin{aligned}
\dfrac{\kappa_{55}}{\kappa_{37}} &= \exp\left[-\dfrac{E_a}{R}\left(\dfrac{1}{328} - \dfrac{1}{310}\right)\right] \\
&= \exp\left[-\dfrac{80{,}000}{8.314}\left(0.00305 - 0.00323\right)\right] \\
&= \exp\left[9623 \times 0.00018\right] \\
&= \exp[1.73] \\
&\approx 5.65
\end{aligned}$$

So at 55 °C, \(t_{1/2}\) drops to roughly \(693 / 5.65 \approx 123\) days.

### 3.3 Step-by-Step: Development of a Starch–PLA Blend

1. **Drying of native starch** at 80 °C for 12 h to reduce moisture below 5 %.
2. **Plasticizer incorporation** — glycerol added at 25–35 wt % relative to starch.
3. **Pre-mixing** in a high-speed mixer at 60 °C for 15 min.
4. **Twin-screw extrusion** — temperature profile 140 / 150 / 160 / 150 / 140 °C; screw speed 120 rpm.
5. **Re-pelletization** with PLA at 70/30, 50/50, 30/70 (starch/PLA) ratios.
6. **Injection molding / film casting** at 165 °C for tensile and barrier testing.
7. **Soil-burial degradation test** as per ISO 17556 — record CO₂ evolution and weight loss.

### 3.4 Analytical Matrix — Hydrolysis vs. Starch Blending

| Performance Metric | Pure PLA | Pure TPS | PLA + 30 % TPS Blend |
|--------------------|----------|----------|----------------------|
| Tensile Strength (MPa) | 50–70 | 3–8 | 25–40 |
| Elongation at Break (%) | 2–10 | 30–80 | 5–15 |
| Water Vapor Permeability | Low | High | Moderate |
| Cost | Moderate–High | Low | Moderate |
| Soil Degradation (months) | 12–24 | 1–3 | 4–10 |
| Hydrolytic Half-life (pH 7, 25 °C) | ~ 24 months | weeks | 6–18 months |

<!-- SECTION_3_END -->

<!-- SECTION_4_START -->
## 4. Structural Diagrams & Schematics

### 4.1 Hydrolytic Degradation Pathway — PLA

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A["PLA Bulk Specimen"] --> B["Water Diffusion Into Matrix"]
    B --> C{"Water Uptake > 1 percent"}
    C -- "Yes" --> D["Ester Bond Cleavage Step 1"]
    D --> E["Formation of Carboxylic Acid End Groups"]
    E --> F{"Acid Catalysis Internal Autoacceleration"}
    F -- "Yes" --> G["Rapid Internal Bulk Erosion"]
    F -- "No" --> H["Uniform Slow Surface Hydrolysis"]
    G --> I["Release of Lactic Acid Oligomers and Monomers"]
    H --> I
    I --> J["Microbial Assimilation to CO2 and H2O"]
    J --> K["Complete Mineralization"]

    style A fill:#cce5ff,stroke:#003366
    style D fill:#ffe5b4,stroke:#cc6600
    style G fill:#ffcccc,stroke:#990000
    style J fill:#ccffcc,stroke:#006600
    style K fill:#99ff99,stroke:#003300
```

### 4.2 Starch-Based Polymer Development — Block Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A["Native Starch Granules"] --> B["Drying at 80 C"]
    B --> C["Addition of Plasticizer Glycerol or Sorbitol"]
    C --> D["Thermomechanical Processing Extrusion"]
    D --> E["Thermoplastic Starch TPS"]
    E --> F{"Processing Route Selection"}
    F -- "Blend Route" --> G["Melt Blending with PLA or PBAT or PCL"]
    F -- "Graft Route" --> H["Graft Copolymerization with Vinyl Monomers"]
    F -- "Composite Route" --> I["Reinforcement with Nanocellulose or Clay"]
    G --> J["Final Product Films or Sheets or Molded Parts"]
    H --> J
    I --> J
    J --> K["Application Packaging or Agriculture or Biomedical"]
    K --> L["End of Life Soil Burial or Composting"]
    L --> M["CO2 plus H2O plus Biomass"]

    style A fill:#fff4cc,stroke:#996600
    style E fill:#ffd9b3,stroke:#cc6600
    style J fill:#cce6ff,stroke:#003366
    style M fill:#ccffcc,stroke:#006600
```

### 4.3 Comparative Hydrolysis Mechanism — Bulk vs. Surface Erosion

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    subgraph SB["Surface Erosion Geometry Preserved"]
        SB1["Outer Eroded Layer"] --> SB2["Intact Inner Core"]
    end

    subgraph BB["Bulk Erosion Internal Cracking Possible"]
        BB1["Water Saturated Matrix"] --> BB2["Random Internal Cleavage"]
        BB2 --> BB3["Oligomer Diffusion Outward"]
    end

    SBE["Mechanism Applies to PGA and High Tg Polymers"] -.-> SB
    BBE["Mechanism Applies to PLA and PCL and PBS"] -.-> BB

    style SB fill:#e6f3ff,stroke:#003366
    style BB fill:#ffe6e6,stroke:#990000
```
